Chapter 6: Casting Spells In lynlisual Conditions 
A wizard's adventures can take him 
to all kinds of exotic locations. But if he 
finds himself battling foes beneath the 
ocean's surface or exploring a mysteri- 
ous locale in an alternate plane of exist- 
ence, he will discover that his spells are 
operating a little differently than us ial. 
This chapter explains the differences. 
Spell casting abilities can also be af- 
fected by sensory changes, such i s  a 
temporary loss of vision, an impa:red 
ability to speak, and impaired move- 
ment. These problems, as well as how a 
wizards spells are affected when cast 
under less than optimum conditions 
and how he might compensate for these 
differences, will be discuss& in this 
chapter.

Casting Spelts in Other 
Planes 
As described on page 132 of the 
Dungeon k t e r ' s  Guide, the AD&D@ 
game world consists of many plae/s of 
existence aside from the familiar Pdime 
Material plane. Just as physical lraws 
operate differently in these planesi so 
do the effects of magic and the ways in 
which spells are cast. General wide- 
lines for using wizardly magic in the 
various planes are given below. (If 
planning a campaign in one or molie of 
the other planes of existence, or if a DM 
is interested in additional information 
about the planes, consult the Manual of 
the Planes.) 
The Ethereal Planes 
From the wizard's perspective, the 
key features of the Ethereal plane4 in- 
clude the following: 
The effects of time occur IO times 
slower than on the Prime Mat$rial 
plane. For instance, for every 10 t+rns 
that pass on the Ethereal planes, odly a 
single turn has passed. Therefore, the 
subjective time for the wizard to rneimo- 
rize and cast spells is 10 times as lodg as 
the actual b
e
 
required. 
The thick soup of ethend mCtter 
restricts clear vision to about 100 yqrds.

the ethereal matter, spells that creatk re- 
straining edfecks, such as web and wall 
of stone, are easily circumvented by the 
victim-he must only move the ethe- 
real matter out of the way. Restraining 
spells that inflict damage, such as wall 
of fire, are equally easy to

Casting Spells In Unusual Conditions 
f 
 
In the plane of Water, spells are af- 
fected as described in the Casting Spells 
Underwater section above. 
In the Energy planes, matter cre 
ated by spells is destroyed one round af- 
ter its creation. Spells that inflict 
variable amounts of damage always in- 
flict maximum damage in the plane of 
Positive Energy and minimum damage 
in the plane of Negative Energy. Spells 
that inflict damage over a period of sev- 
eral rounds function for only one 
rounc 
The schools of magx are attected as 
follows: 
Abjuratiox AU spells of this school 
function normally, subject to the special 
laws of the inner planes, except where 
noted in the spell description. Spells af- 
fecting extradimensional matures do 
not affect matures native to the inner 
planes. When the anti-magic shell is used 
in the inner planes, all magical protec- 
tions are removed from within that 
sphere, removing any protection the 
user may have to survive in that plane. 
Alteration: These spells are the most 
likely to be modified as described 
above. A lower water spell, for in- 
stance, can be modified to lower fire in 
the plane of Fire, and airy water could 
be modified to airy fire. 
Here are a few special cases: 
Transmute rock to mud can func- 
tion as transform element to para- 
element; earth becomes ooze or 
magma, fire becomes magma or smoke, 
air becomes smoke or ice, and water be- 
comes ice or ooze. Each type of trans- 
mutation 
must 
be 
researched 
separately. 
Transmute water to dust can be 
modified to transform element to nega- 
tiue quasi-element; elemental water be- 
comes ash, air becomes vacuum, fire 
becomes ash, and earth becomes dust. 
Astral spell will not work in the in- 
ner planes. 
Conjuration/Summoning: Usually, 
spells summoning a particular type of 
elemental creature summon that being 
only in its native plane. These spells do 
not guarantee control over the sum- 
moned elemental, but the elemental is 
not immediately hostile to the caster. 
Spells summoning animals, mon- 
sters, or other Prime Material creatures 
instead summon the elemental versions 
of these creatures. Elemental creatures 
have the same appearance and abilities 
as their Prime Material counterparts, 
but are elementals of that type found in 
that inner plane (except that the Posi- 
tive and Negative planes have no ele- 
mental types). 
Spells that contact b e i i  in the As- 
tral and outer planes do not function in 
the inner planes. 
Divination: These spells function 
normally. Those that call on an entity 
function only if that entity is native to 
the inner planes or Ethereal planes. 
EnchantmenUCharrn: These spells 
function normally. Hold person and 
charm spells work only against human- 
oid elementals. 
ILlusionlPhantasm: These spells func- 
tion normally. 
InvocationlEvocation: Usually, invc- 
cation spells function only if the entity's 
domain is in the same plane as the w
